Title: Reconstitution of monoterpene indole alkaloid biosynthesis in genome engineered Nicotiana benthamiana

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Off-target host-derived dead-end derivatives that reduce pathway cleanliness
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endogenous UGT; identified from leaf transcriptome; high normalized counts following nepetalactol-pathway expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endogenous leaf expression responsive to pathway/agroinfiltration context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration and expression of geraniol/nepetalactol pathway intermediates altered endogenous UGT expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics-guided target selection; Cas9-mediated targeted mutagenesis; UHPLC/MS metabolite profiling in infiltrated mutant lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NbUGT94E7 eliminated accumulation of trihexosyl geranic acid and also eliminated acetyl dihexosyl geraniol in the nepetalactol-producing context, but did not improve strictosidine yield
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transcriptomics and phylogeny nominated NbUGT94E7 as a candidate endogenous derivatizing enzyme. 2. Cas9 knockout lines lacked a specific derivative peak assigned as trihexosyl geranic acid. 3. Removing NbUGT94E7 reduced early pathway derivatization but had no detectable effect on final strictosidine yield.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful host chassis-editing target to reduce off-target glycosylation and improve product profile of early monoterpene intermediates
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth or developmental defects were observed in edited lines
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This UGT lacks the GSS motif proposed to define the loop structure of monoglucosyltransferases

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endogenous leaf UGT; previously reported as active on geraniol
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endogenous leaf expression in pathway/chassis context
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agroinfiltration and heterologous iridoid pathway expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cas9-mediated targeted mutagenesis and UHPLC/MS metabolite profiling in mutant lines expressing geraniol or nepetalactol pathway modules
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation of NbUGT85A73 reduced or eliminated several glycosylated derivatives including hexosyl hydroxygeraniol, hexosyl hydroxycitronellal, pentosyl hexosyl geraniol, and acetyl dihexosyl geraniol, especially in the nepetalactol-pathway context; no improvement in strictosidine yield
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbUGT85A73 was identified as an endogenous UGT acting on early iridoid/geraniol-derived intermediates. 2. Mutant lines accumulated fewer specific glycosylated derivatives. 3. Despite a cleaner early metabolite profile, final strictosidine production was unchanged.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising host-editing target for reducing background derivatization of heterologous monoterpene products
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth or developmental defects were observed in edited lines
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Effects were strongest when pathway enzymes through nepetalactol were expressed; the gene had previously been reported as active on geraniol

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central biosynthetic intermediate of all known monoterpene indole alkaloids, including many medicinal compounds
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Iridoid cyclization module leading to nepetalactol/7-deoxyloganic acid formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Reactive enol intermediate generated by iridoid synthase
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Enhanced formation of cis-trans nepetalactol/7-deoxyloganic acid pathway product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assists/couples the cyclization of the reactive enol produced by iridoid synthase, improving formation of the stereochemistry required for the strictosidine pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point/bottleneck-relief enzyme for flux through the iridoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstitution by transient co-expression; metabolite profiling by UPLC/MS using 7-DLA and strictosidine standards; omission/addition comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Inclusion of MLPL1 from Nepeta mussinii enabled detectable 7-deoxyloganic acid production in N. benthamiana. 2. MLPL1 was critical for overcoming the iridoid pathway bottleneck and enabling de novo strictosidine biosynthesis. 3. Addition of MLPL1 increased strictosidine production by more than 60-fold.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes flux through the iridoid pathway and is essential for efficient strictosidine accumulation in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme to include in heterologous strictosidine/MIA pathway engineering; should be stacked with the core secoiridoid pathway to overcome cyclization bottlenecks
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states this enzyme is critical for improving flux through the iridoid pathway and essential for 7-DLA production in this host

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central biosynthetic intermediate of all known monoterpene indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP-derived monoterpene precursor supply for strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces geranyl diphosphate to supply monoterpene precursor for geraniol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in leaves; plastid targeting improved output
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient pathway co-expression with and without PaGPPS; strictosidine quantification by UPLC/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaGPPS was included to enhance the GPP pool for the plastidial MEP pathway. 2. Supplementation with PaGPPS improved strictosidine yield about 5-fold. 3. Chloroplast-targeted PaGPPS increased precursor availability for geraniol formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases strictosidine yield by boosting GPP precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply module for monoterpene/MIA engineering, especially with plastid targeting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a supportive precursor-supply enzyme rather than a native dedicated strictosidine enzyme

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Central biosynthetic intermediate of all known monoterpene indole alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: ER membrane-associated in native pathway; cytosolic/ER-facing in optimal reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes geraniol at C8 to form 8-hydroxygeraniol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established here as rate-limiting, but important early dedicated step
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient co-expression in leaves; compartmentation affected pathway yield
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stepwise pathway reconstruction and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrG8H functioned as an early dedicated pathway enzyme in the reconstructed pathway. 2. Its inclusion enabled progression from geraniol toward iridoid intermediates. 3. Proper subcellular localization was important for efficient pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of geraniol into downstream iridoid intermediates required for strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential component for heterologous MIA pathway assembly; should be co-optimized with CYP reductase context and compartmentation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes poor G8H expression has limited strictosidine production in yeast, but not a main experimental focus here

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stepwise pathway reconstruction and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrGOR was part of the reconstituted early iridoid pathway. 2. Its activity supported production of nepetalactol-pathway intermediates and downstream strictosidine. 3. It was one of the core enzymes co-expressed in successful pathway assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables flux from hydroxylated geraniol intermediates toward cyclization substrates for strictosidine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required in pathway stacks for heterologous MIA production

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reactive enol intermediate leading to nepetalactol
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 8-oxogeranial to a reactive enol intermediate that undergoes cyclization in the iridoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Bottleneck-associated step relieved by MLPL1 assistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstitution with metabolite profiling; comparison with/without MLPL1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrISY generated the reactive intermediate for iridoid ring formation. 2. Efficient downstream flux in N. benthamiana depended on MLPL1-assisted cyclization of the ISY product. 3. CrISY was a core enzyme in successful strictosidine reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for production of iridoid intermediates leading to strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Must be paired with MLPL1 or related cyclization-assisting enzymes in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames the bottleneck at the cyclization stage downstream of ISY product formation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to 7-deoxyloganetic acid to produce 7-deoxyloganic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Omission/addition experiment and 7-DLA LC-MS comparison with standard
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Inclusion of Cr7-DLGT enabled authentic 7-DLA peak formation. 2. Excluding Cr7-DLGT generated a different peak likely due to endogenous UGT activity on 7-deoxyloganetic acid. 3. Strong heterologous expression of Cr7-DLGT outcompeted native glycosylation activities.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes correct formation of 7-DLA and suppresses formation of an alternative endogenous glucose ester peak
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important to include for pathway specificity in heterologous hosts prone to endogenous glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrates pathway-specific glycosylation can outcompete host background metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts loganin to secologanin in the late secoiridoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a likely bottleneck step in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequential pathway addition with intermediate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loganin was the only major intermediate observed to accumulate in the full pathway. 2. This suggested secologanin synthase is a bottleneck step in N. benthamiana reconstruction. 3. CrSLS is required to convert loganin to the strictosidine precursor secologanin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Limits downstream conversion of loganin and likely constrains strictosidine yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Priority late-pathway target for optimization in future strictosidine/MIA engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper contrasts this with prior suggestions that LAMT may be rate limiting

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Universal precursor to >3000 MIAs with broad medicinal relevance
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol/vacuole-associated native context; heterologously expressed in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses secologanin with tryptamine to form strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full pathway reconstitution and matching LC-MS peak to strictosidine standard
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of the full pathway including STR produced a peak matching a strictosidine standard. 2. The reconstructed pathway yielded 4.29 ± 2.00 μM strictosidine, equivalent to 0.23 ± 0.11 mg/g dry weight. 3. STR catalyzed the terminal pathway step to the universal MIA intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly enables strictosidine accumulation in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal enzyme for plug-and-play assembly of downstream MIA modules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: A minor likely malonylated strictosidine derivative accumulated due to endogenous host acyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strictosidine is relatively stable/polar compared with upstream hydrophobic iridoid intermediates
